sourceMovie.title: The Holdovers
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Holdovers (2023) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Forrest Gump
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Holdovers clicked for you, Forrest Gump (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Holdovers?
faq.answer: Try Forrest Gump, Stuck in Love, The Diary of a Teenage Girl—each shares DNA with The Holdovers while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Holdovers?
faq.answer: The Holdovers (2023) is best described as Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Holdovers worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
